premises.premise: The structural similarity of $HgBa_{2}CuO_{4+\delta}$ (Hg-1201) to the thallium-containing compound $TlBa_{2}CuO_{5}$ (Tl-1201) suggests the existence of mercury-based compounds with the general composition $HgBa_{2}Ca_{n-1}Cu_{n}O_{2n+2+\delta}$, analogous to the thallium family $TlBa_{2}Ca_{n-1}Cu_{n}O_{2n+3}$. [1, 4, 5]

The sample material was prepared from a precursor with nominal composition $Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{5}$ mixed with powdered $HgO$, sealed in evacuated quartz tubes, heated at $800^{\circ}C$ for 5 hours (during which the tubes broke), and subsequently annealed for 5 hours at $300^{\circ}C$ in flowing oxygen.

Energy-dispersive X-ray spectrometry (EDS) analysis shows that the synthesized samples are composites containing isolated grains of $BaCuO_{2}$ ($\sim30\%$), $CuO$ ($\approx30\%$), oxides with Ca/Cu, impurities, and approximately 15% of the total sample volume consisting of plate-like grains containing Hg, Ba, Ca, and Cu.

Selected-area electron-diffraction (SAED) and high-resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM) on the plate-like grains show that they consist of pure $HgBa_{2}Ca_{2}Cu_{3}O_{8+x}$ (Hg-1223), disordered mixtures of Hg-1223 and $HgBa_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{6+x}$ (Hg-1212), and periodic stacking sequences of these unit cells.
The volume fraction of the phases of interest is too small to measure the lattice parameters precisely with the X-ray Guinier technique.

High-resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM) images reveal stacking sequences containing both Hg-1212 and Hg-1223 layers, specifically a sequence 1223/1223/1212/1212/1223/1213 forming a supercell with a c-axis $c \approx 86.4 \AA$.

reasoning.step: Based on the structural similarity to thallium-based copper oxides, the authors hypothesize the existence of the Hg-based homologous series $HgBa_{2}Ca_{n-1}Cu_{n}O_{2n+2+\delta}$, specifically expecting phases corresponding to $n=2$ (Hg-1212) and $n=3$ (Hg-1223).
The authors synthesized a black material using the precursor method described in [@premise-2].
Using EDS, the authors identified that ~15% of the synthesized material consists of plate-like grains containing the target elements Hg, Ba, Ca, and Cu [@premise-3].

The authors investigated these specific grains using SAED and HRTEM, confirming the presence of distinct Hg-1223 and Hg-1212 phases, as well as disordered mixtures and periodic stackings [@premise-4].
Due to the insufficient volume fraction for precise X-ray diffraction analysis [@premise-5], the authors utilized SAED patterns to deduce the lattice constants.
From the SAED patterns, the authors calculated the tetragonal lattice parameters to be $a=3.93(7)\AA$ for both phases, $c=12.7(2)\AA$ for the Hg-1212 units, and $c=16.1(3)\AA$ for the Hg-1223 units.

The authors further confirmed the existence of ordered superstructures through HRTEM imaging, which displayed a periodic stacking of Hg-1212 and Hg-1223 units with a supercell c-axis of $c \approx 86.4 \AA$ [@premise-6].

conclusion.title: Identification of Hg-1212 and Hg-1223 phases and lattice parameters

inference_unit.conclusion: The synthesized material contains two distinct high-temperature superconducting phases: $HgBa_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{6+x}$ (Hg-1212, with two $CuO_{2}$ layers) and $HgBa_{2}Ca_{2}Cu_{3}O_{8+x}$ (Hg-1223, with three $CuO_{2}$ layers), as well as ordered superstructures (e.g., a supercell with $c \approx 86.4 \AA$). The tetragonal lattice parameters derived from selected-area electron diffraction (SAED) are $a=3.93(7)\AA$ for both phases, with $c=12.7(2)\AA$ for Hg-1212 and $c=16.1(3)\AA$ for Hg-1223.
